issues: panel occupies ~27% scene width, moderately large but contextually acceptable for a tutorial card
internal item illustrations appear slightly dense/small within card bounds, reducing readability at game scale
reasoning: The controls_card_v00 panel is a UI overlay tutorial card composited centrally over the top-down scene. As a 'panel' category it is not subject to world-entity size rules (player 32px baseline), but is evaluated for scene proportion and toy-diorama fit. The card spans roughly 350x175px against a 1280x680 scene (~7% area), which is reasonable for a controls instruction popup. Pixel art aesthetic inside the card (illustrated items, scroll border) matches the game's toy-diorama style well. No perspective inconsistency — flat UI panel reads correctly as an overlay. Minor concerns: the card is on the larger end for a UI panel and occludes a significant central gameplay area; the illustrated items inside the card are rendered quite small relative to card area, making them hard to read. No creature/item world-size violations visible. No 'wallpaper' or 'zoomed-in' distortion. Marginal rather than pass due to panel size and dense internal layout.
